channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Another unticketed passenger attempts to stow away on a Delta flight
item.description: In the thick of holiday travel, another unticketed passenger attempted to stow away on a Delta Air Lines flight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to delay TikTok ban so he can weigh in after he takes office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump asked the Supreme Court on Friday to pause the potential TikTok ban from going into effect until his administration can pursue a “political resolution” to the issue.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KRON4

item.title: Final crystal triangles installed on Times Square ball ahead of New Year's Eve
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The last of the crystal triangles that make up this year's Times Square New Year's Eve ball were installed on Friday morning, marking the first time in 10 years that all 2,688 were replaced at once.
item.dc:creator: BY MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News hosts Sean Hannity, Ainsley Earhardt announce engagement
item.description: "We are overjoyed and so thankful to our families for all of their love and support during this wonderful time in our lives," the couple said.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: US to send $1.25 billion in weapons to Ukraine, pushing to get aid out before Biden leaves office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is expected to announce that it will send $1.25 billion in military assistance to Ukraine, U.S. officials said Friday, as the Biden administration pushes to get as much aid to Kyiv as possible before leaving office on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Onion-wielding man disrupts Christmas church services, pours whiskey into holy water: deputies
item.description: A Maryland man was arrested and charged with several offences stemming from disruptions at two Catholic churches on Christmas Eve, including one disruption which he is accused of pouring liquor into a container of holy water.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: The State Department says an American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than four years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In states that ban abortion, social safety net programs often fail families
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Taylor Cagnacci moved from California to Tennessee with hopes of starting a new chapter in a state that touts a low cost of living and natural beauty.
item.dc:creator: LAURA UNGAR and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe survives close brush with the sun's scorching surface
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — NASA's Parker Solar Probe has successfully made the closest approach to the sun, the space agency confirmed Friday. Earlier this week, the spacecraft passed within a record-breaking 3.8 million miles (6 million kilometers) of the scorching star.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SEE IT: ‘Peanut-shaped’ roundabout ready to open in Ohio
item.description: In a post to the city's Facebook page, officials offered "a few pointers to help drivers navigate safely.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Retailers, chains that announced closures in 2024
item.description: It was another tough year for restaurants and traditional brick-and-mortar retailers. Several national chains announced closures and bankruptcies in 2024 – not to mention the local small businesses that quietly shuttered around the country.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Woman says to-be fiancé died in Christmas house fire searching for ring
item.description: A California man died in a Christmas morning house fire while searching for an engagement ring for his girlfriend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Peyote sacred to Native Americans threatened by psychedelic renaissance and development
item.description: HEBBRONVILLE, Texas (AP) — In this corner of southern Texas, the plump cacti seem to pop out of arid dust and cracked earth, like magic dumplings.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH and JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cat food sold in California, 11 other states recalled following death
item.description: An Oregon house cat died after eating pet food that tested positive for bird flu, Oregon authorities said, prompting a recall of raw frozen pet food that was sold nationwide.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Cat food distributed to 12 states recalled after positive bird flu test; 1 cat dead
item.description: The product was sold through distributors in Arizona, California, Colorado, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Maryland, Michigan, Minnesota, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island and Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trial of man accused in Trump assassination attempt in Florida pushed back to September
item.description: A man accused of attempting to assassinate President-elect Donald Trump in South Florida won't be tried until September 2025, a federal judge ruled this week.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jackpot for Mega Millions over $1 billion after no numbers match on Christmas Eve drawing
item.description: Hang onto those stocking-stuffer lottery tickets. The Mega Millions jackpot has surpassed $1 billion after no ticket matched all six numbers drawn on Christmas Eve. That means the estimated jackpot for Friday's drawing is $1.15 billion, potentially the fifth-largest jackpot in the game's history.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Major storm pounds California's central coast, blamed for man's death and partially collapsing pier
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A major storm pounded California’s central coast on Monday, bringing flooding and high surf that was blamed for fatally trapping a man beneath debris on a beach and later partially collapsing a pier, tossing three people into the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Owl flies into Virginia home, lands atop Christmas tree
item.description: One family in Virginia got quite the surprise on Wednesday, Dec. 18, when a barred owl flew into their home through the chimney and ended up perched on their Christmas tree.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Judge rules Arkansas law allowing criminal charges against librarians is unconstitutional
item.description: FAYETTEVILLE, Ark. (AP) — A federal judge on Monday struck down key parts of an Arkansas law that would have allowed criminal charges against librarians and booksellers for providing “harmful” materials to minors. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bill Clinton admitted to hospital with fever
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton was admitted to a Washington hospital on Monday with a fever, his spokesperson said on social media.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Inside the state and federal charges against the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A triptych of criminal charges paints a searing, sometimes disparate portrait of the man accused of ambushing and killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO Brian Thompson as the executive arrived at a Manhattan hotel for his company’s annual investor conference.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Third entangled endangered whale spotted in span of a week off East Coast
item.description: A third endangered whale has been spotted entangled in fishing gear off the East Coast, marking an alarming end to the year for a species threatened with extinction.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Civil rights icon James Meredith is honored in his Mississippi hometown
item.description: KOSCIUSKO, Miss. (AP) — A new historical marker has been unveiled in the hometown of James Meredith, honoring the Black man who fought white supremacy by integrating the University of Mississippi in 1962.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: Albanian authorities held 1,300 meetings with teachers and parents following the stabbing death of a teenager in mid-November by another teen after a quarrel that started on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, WGN 9
